observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that timely and accurate
RPT assessments are made through managing the factors affecting the availability of
updated real property valuations by initiating, where practicable through its
Sanggunian, the periodic revision of fair market values of real properties within the
Municipality; immediately implementing an enacted SFMV; and adequately
backstopping such prompt implementation of SFMV with adequate manpower and
prompt compliance to precursor processes.

We recommended and Management agreed to correct the RPT and SEF income earned in 2021 which was apparently included in 2022. Accordingly,
Management effected the following proposed adjusting journal entry (PAJE):
Accounts Debit Credit
RPT/SEF Income 134,694.02
Prior Period Adjustment (PPA) 134,694.02
To derecognized the RPT and SEF income earned in 2021.

On other matters discussed, we recommended and Management agreed, with the Office of the Municipal
Treasurer as lead, to enhance its operating practices to further reinforce controls over its RPT and SEF
collections through institutionalizing measures that would ensure that RPT and SEF collections are seasonably
remitted by collectors, and immediately deposited to an AGDB.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Offices of the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer as leads, to enhance the reliability of its
financial representation by instituting arrangements to ensure that RPT/SEF
receivables and collections are appropriately recognized and recorded.

We recommended and Management agreed to correct the noted error in recording
employees’ loans with LBP as Loans Receivable. Accordingly, the Management
effected the following PAJE:
Accounts Debit Credit
Due from Officers and Employees 38,091,661.66
Loans Receivable 38,091,661.66
To reclassify the employees’ loans to its proper account.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Offices of the Municipal
Mayor and Accountant as leads, to strictly enforce the proper deduction of loans from
the concerned LGU employees, with due observance with the existing legal provision
on net take home pay; and exert utmost efforts to collect the amounts due from
employees who have already retired/separated from the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer as leads, with
backstopping from the LCE, to establish necessary operating
modalities that will effectively address concerns affecting the
submission of liquidation reports to funding agencies in order to
reinforce accountability and transparency.
observations.reason: (This pertains to the current administration only.)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed, through the
Municipal GAD FPS-TWG with the GAD Focal Person as
lead, to enhance the quality and delivery of its GAD initiatives
by:
a. adequately and appropriately establishing, Fully
maintaining, and keeping up-to-date its GAD Implemented
Database through the implementation and utilization
of the Community-Based Monitoring System and
other practicable data gathering processes/tools; and
b. improving institutional knowledgebase through Fully
capacity building of GAD actors on Implemented
nuances/matters/principles/technicalities such as, but
not limited to, risk-base management, result-based
management, project management, and gender-issue
identification that affect GBP planning,
implementation, monitoring, evaluation, and
reporting.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the
Municipal Accountant, and with the appropriate intervention
and support from the LCE, to adopt necessary and
practicable measures to exact barangay compliance with the
submission of financial documents and related financial
reports in order to bolster transparency and accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed through the
MDRRMO, to:
a. immediately adopt relevant measures in collecting Fully
data needed for the preparation of LDRRMP. If Implemented
necessary, and if budget is available, request funding
for the adoption and conduct of the CBMS so as to
have the necessary database in preparing all relevant
plans of the municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed:
1. through the Municipal Development Council and
Municipal Planning and Development Office:
a. through the implementing departments concerned, to Fully
maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Implemented
Fund so that all of its programmed development
projects could be implemented on time to ensure that
intended beneficiaries can avail of the maximum
benefits that could be derived therefrom;
b. through the Municipal Accountant, to maintain a Partially Special account has
special account in the General Fund for development Implemented already been
projects from the 20% of the IRA. Also, separate established but
financial statements should be prepared pursuant to financial statements are
Section 110, Volume 1 of the New Government yet to be made.
Accounting System Manual to efficiently monitor the
actual utilization of the fund.

Special account has already been established but

GSO-designate, to:

to the acquisition of PPEs in prior years in order to
start the reconciliation process. In addition, prepare
the appropriate cards of those PPEs that had been
identified already in order to start the compliance
process of preparing such monitoring tool.

We recommended the Management, through the MENRO, to Ongoing
fast track the construction of the Municipality’s Sanitary
Landfill in order to address the pressing issue on waste
disposal within the locality, in compliance with existing Rules
and Regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB, Fully
to enhance accountability of the acquired and issued assets Implemented
and/or properties by conducting regular physical inventory of
the same, and evaluating on a timely basis the status and
issuance of these subject assets to monitor their whereabouts
and serviceability.

We recommended and Management concurred to require the Fully
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to remit intact all the Implemented
withheld amounts and social mandatory
contributions/deductions to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and (This pertains to the
PhilHealth within the prescribed reglementary period. current administration

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management concurred to reorient
themselves on the government procurement and its IRR and
ensure that all procurement transactions/activities are in
accordance with the prescribed procedures under 2016
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184
such as the following:
a. In all instances of procurement, the BAC shall strictly Fully
adhere to the advertisement and posting Implemented
requirements prescribed in Section 21 of RA 9184;
b. Strictly adhere to the guidelines prescribed in Annex Fully
H of IRR, RA 9184 for the alternative methods of Implemented
procurement; and
c. Refrain from purchasing goods and services through Fully
reimbursements and that procurement of common Implemented
use goods and equipment should be procured in the
PS-DBM, if available or in bulk quantities, to avoid
piecemeal purchases. Purchasing in bulk ultimately
results to savings for the Municipality in terms of
lower costs, less time and effort devoted to the process
of procurement, inspection and acceptance, and
corresponding disbursement.

We recommended and Management concurred to require all Fully
Office Heads and Drivers to submit regularly through the Implemented
Municipal Treasurer, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption
and Monthly Report of Official Travels for proper control
and monitoring.

We recommended and Management concurred to:
1. Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory Fully
method of accounting for all supplies and materials Implemented
procured. Also, maintain subsidiary ledger cards for each
item of inventory; and
2. Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain stock cards and Fully
property cards for supplies and materials; (ii) prepare Implemented
and submit monthly the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued to the Accounting Office for the
recording of issuances.

We recommended and Management concurred to instruct
the:
1. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Fully
Accounting Office with the Certified List of Taxpayers Implemented
and tax due and collectible every beginning of the year;
and
2. Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT Fully
and SET Receivables based on the certified list from the Implemented
Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management concurred to direct the Fully
concerned official to strictly observe Sections 271 and 164 of Implemented
RA 7160 on the remittance of shares on Real Property and
Community Taxes to avoid legal repercussions that might be
raised by the monitoring government agency and legally
mandated recipients.

We recommended and Management concurred to submit all Ongoing
liquidation reports and ensure that these are supported with
complete and proper documents prescribed under COA
Circular 2012-001.

We recommended and Management concurred to require the Fully
accountant to reconcile ledger balances against Treasurer’s Implemented
cashbook and prepare the necessary Reconciliation Statement
pursuant to Section 181 (c) Government Accounting and
Auditing Manual, Volume I.

a. Review the authority granted to officials and Fully
employees and consider the necessity, reasonableness, Implemented
and rank in granting these allowances. Be guided by

annexes.
b. Lastly, consider the different offers from different
telecommunication companies that will suit the needs Fully
of the municipal government to be able to save and Implemented

1. The General Services Officer together with the Ongoing
Municipal Engineer must conduct an inventory of local
roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction. Provide a copy of
the inventory reports to the accountant for proper
accounting and reconciliation; and
2. The municipal accountant shall reconcile the inventory Ongoing
report with those recorded in the registry of public
infrastructure. Also, the accountant should strictly
follow the accounting and reporting procedures
provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the
valuation of these road asset networks.

The Management should furnish the Auditor with the copies
of contracts on infrastructure projects, and purchase orders Fully
on procured goods and services, with all the documents Implemented
forming part thereof, within five (5) working days from their
perfections and issuances pursuant to the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended and Management concurred to:
1. Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory
method of accounting for all supplies and materials available as of date.
Also, maintain subsidiary ledger cards for each
item of inventory; and
2. Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain stock cards and
property cards for supplies and materials; (ii) prepare
and submit monthly the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued to the Accounting Office for the
recording of issuances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Engineer as leads, with backstopping from the LCE, to establish
necessary operating modalities that will effectively address concerns affecting the
submission of liquidation reports to funding agencies in order to reinforce accountability
and transparency.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal GAD FPS-
TWG with the GAD Focal Person as lead, to enhance the quality and delivery of its GAD
initiatives by:

a. adequately and appropriately establishing, maintaining, and keeping up-to-
date its GAD Database through the implementation and utilization of the
Community-Based Monitoring System and other practicable data gathering
processes/tools; and

b. improving institutional knowledgebase through capacity building of GAD
actors on nuances/matters/principles/technicalities such as, but not limited
to, risk-base management, result-based management, project management,
and gender-issue identification that affect GBP planning, implementation,
monitoring, evaluation, and reporting.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accountant,
and with the appropriate intervention and support from the LCE, to adopt necessary and
practicable measures to exact barangay compliance with the submission of financial
documents and related financial reports in order to bolster transparency and
accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGUs of the province further enhance the quality of its individual LDRRM management processes by taking concrete actions to address the issues related to manpower, data and information, and formalization to reinforce the establishment of an enabling level of coordination in a collective setting and ensure disaster resiliency.
We recommended and Management agreed through the MDRRMO, to:

We recommended and Management agreed:
1. through the Municipal Development Council and
Municipal Planning and Development Office, to:
a. ensure that the appropriation for 20% development
projects is supported by a listing of specific
programs, projects and activities to be undertaken to
facilitate proper evaluation of its appropriateness
pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations; and
b. evaluate the appropriateness of PPAs funded out of
the 20% DF and refrain from allocating funds other
than those expressly provided by the guidelines.
2. through the implementing departments concerned, to
maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so
that all of its programmed development projects could be
implemented on time to ensure that intended beneficiaries
can avail of the maximum benefits that could be derived
therefrom;
3. through the Municipal Accountant, to maintain a special
account in the General Fund for development projects from
the 20% of the IRA. Also, separate financial statements
should be prepared pursuant to Section 110, Volume 1 of
the New Government Accounting System Manual to
efficiently monitor the actual utilization of the fund; and
4. through the Municipal Engineering Office, to prepare the
Quarterly Report on Government Publicized Projects,
Projects and Activities as set forth by the existing
regulations.
observations.reason:
Special account has already been established but
financial statements are yet to be made.

Implemented since the matter was subsumed in the current finding on
Fund Transfers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed through the
GSO-designate, to:
a. adopt immediate measures as regards the formation
of the inventory committee in order to ascertain the
whereabouts and condition of the properties.
Likewise, if the committee had been formed, advise
the committee to regularly perform their designated
functions in accordance with what has been
mandated of them in order to ascertain the correct
balances as well as the whereabouts of the PPEs. If
warranted, facilitate the immediate disposition of all
unserviceable properties in order to cleanse the PPE
accounts;
b. strictly adhere to the guidelines set by the Handbook
on Property and Supply Management and other
related issuances on sound practices, such as but not
limited to setting an effective internal control
measures over PPEs to protect such from possible
mishandling and/or loss of property; and
c. fast track the retrieval of the documents pertaining
to the acquisition of PPEs in prior years in order to
start the reconciliation process. In addition, prepare
the appropriate cards of those PPEs that had been
identified already in order to start the compliance
process of preparing such monitoring tool.

We recommended the Management, through the MENRO, to:
a. fast track the construction of the Municipality’s
Sanitary Landfill in order to address the pressing
issue on waste disposal within the locality, in
compliance with existing Rules and Regulations;
b. as to the programs in minimizing solid wastes, review
the Programs under each strategy for viability, and if
warranted, revise the plan to ensure doability and
sustainability, taking into consideration the possible
hindrances on non-implementation. In addition,
check the budget allocation for each program to
ensure that enough resources are given to every
program; and
c. exert extra effort in monitoring the Municipal
Government’s compliance with Laws, Rules and
Regulations with regards to Solid Waste
Management.
observations.reason:
The site for the sanitary landfill was already
identified, however, there were some issued
as regards the ownership of the land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB,
to:
1. observe timely preparation and submission of the SEF
Annual Budget as provided under existing Rules and
Regulations;
2. improve the processes of planning and budgeting of SEF
through:
a. instituting measures to make the LSB more proactive
and effective in introducing educational reforms
within its capacity; and
b. the formulation of development programs on
education for the Municipal Government,
articulating the collective vision of the major
stakeholders;
3. enhance the administration and collection of SEF through
crafting of an effective tax campaign that could
significantly maximize collections in order to improve and
make reforms on the quality of education within locality.
4. enhance accountability of the acquired and issued assets
and/or properties by conducting regular physical
inventory of the same, and evaluating on a timely basis the
status and issuance of these subject assets to monitor their
whereabouts and serviceability; and
5. revisit the relevant guidelines on SEF and related rules
and regulations on property, supply and inventory
management.

We recommended and Management concurred to require the
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to remit intact all the
withheld amounts and social mandatory
contributions/deductions to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and
PhilHealth within the prescribed reglementary period.

We recommended and Management concurred to reorient
themselves on the government procurement and its IRR and
ensure that all procurement transactions/activities are in
accordance with the prescribed procedures under 2016
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184
such as the following:
a. All procurement should be in accordance with the
approved APP. Make sure that the APP were
carefully planned and scheduled to better manage the
resources, produce economical result, make the
procurement procedures more efficient and practical
in the part of the government and do away with
redundancy;
b. In all instances of procurement, the BAC shall strictly
adhere to the advertisement and posting
requirements prescribed in Section 21 of RA 9184;
c. Strictly adhere to the guidelines prescribed in Annex
H of IRR, RA 9184 for the alternative methods of
procurement; and
d. Refrain from purchasing goods and services through
reimbursements and that procurement of common
use goods and equipment should be procured in the
PS-DBM, if available or in bulk quantities, to avoid
piecemeal purchases. Purchasing in bulk ultimately
results to savings for the Municipality in terms of
lower costs, less time and effort devoted to the process
of procurement, inspection and acceptance, and
corresponding disbursement.

We recommended and Management concurred to require all
Office Heads and Drivers to submit regularly through the
Municipal Treasurer, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption
and Monthly Report of Official Travels for proper control
and monitoring.

We recommended and Management concurred to instruct
the:
1. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the
Accounting Office with the Certified List of Taxpayers
and tax due and collectible every beginning of the year; and
2. Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT
and SET Receivables based on the certified list from the
Treasury Office.

We recommended and management concurred to direct the
concerned official to strictly observe Sections 271 and 164 of
RA 7160 on the remittance of shares on Real Property and
Community Taxes to avoid legal repercussions that might be
raised by the monitoring government agency and legally
mandated recipients.

We recommended and Management concurred to ensure that
the concerned official follow strictly the laws, rules and
regulations on the submission of accounts, otherwise, institute
legal actions for non-rendition of accounts within the
prescribed deadlines.

We recommended and Management concurred to:
a. Direct all concerned accountable officers to settle all
their outstanding cash advances, otherwise institute
sanctions by withholding the salaries of those who
failed to liquidate their cash advances and by not
granting additional cash advances unless the previous
ones have been liquidated;
b. Enforce strict compliance to rules and regulations
pertaining to grant and utilization of cash advances
pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular
2009-002; and
c. Submit all liquidation reports and ensure that these
are supported with complete and proper documents
prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended and Management concurred to require the
accountant to reconcile ledger balances against Treasurer’s
cashbook and prepare the necessary Reconciliation Statement
pursuant to Section 181 (c) Government Accounting and
Auditing Manual, Volume I.

We recommended and Management concurred that the 20% Development Fund be treated as special accounts in the General Fund books pursuant to Section 313 of Republic Act 7160. Separate financial statements should also be prepared pursuant to Section 110, Volume 1 of the New Government Accounting System Manual to efficiently monitor the actual utilization of the fund. In addition, request to Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Implemented since the matter was subsumed in the 2020 AAR

observations.recommendation:
a. The concerned municipal officials and employees concerned should immediately stop the granting of cash advances to any officials and employees unless the previous cash advance given is first settled.
b. The municipal officials should stop the practice of implementation of projects through drawing of cash advances and follow the proper procurement process as provided under R.A. 9184.
c. The management should exercise diligence in accounting, settlement and monitoring of outstanding cash advances by requiring all accountable officers concerned to refund, submit documents and/or make a proper accounting of their outstanding cash advances, and establish a policy on monitoring of cash advances to ensure that immediate action is taken to prevent recurrence of unliquidated cash advances.
d. Lastly, submit to the audit team all liquidation reports concerning the implementation of programs and projects from the cash advances drawn by the municipal officials for both current and prior years.
observations.reason: Implemented since the matter was subsumed in the 2020 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the LGUs of the province
further enhance the quality of its individual LDRRM management processes by
taking concrete actions to address the issues related to manpower, data and
information, and formalization to reinforce the establishment of an enabling level of
coordination in a collective setting and ensure disaster resiliency.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the MDRRMO, to
immediately adopt relevant measures in collecting data needed for the preparation
of LDRRMP. If necessary, and if budget is available, request funding for the
adoption and conduct of the CBMS so as to have the necessary database in
preparing the all relevant plans of the municipality;

We recommended and Management agreed, through the MDRRMO, to:
a) revisit the guidelines relative to the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure
and enforce the strict compliance thereof. Likewise, review and assess the
programs, projects and activities with respect to their compliance with
existing laws, rules and regulations as a requirement before these
undertakings are endeavored to form part of the LDRRMFIP; and
b) identify programs, projects and activities, with a view of the funding
constraints, by assessing which to prioritize and implement vis-vis the
LGU determined disaster related issues;

We recommended and Management agreed, through the MDRRMO, to
review the guidelines in updating the LDRRMFIP to ensure that relevant
measures are properly observed. Likewise, evaluate the compliance of the
LGU as regards the appropriation procedures to ensure that proper
authorization had been complied first before the commencement of the
disbursement process.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal
Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Office, to:
1. ensure that the appropriation for 20% development projects is supported by
a listing of specific programs, projects and activities to be undertaken
to facilitate proper evaluation of its appropriateness pursuant to existing
laws, rules and regulations; and
2. evaluate the appropriateness of PPAs funded out of the 20% DF and
refrain from allocating funds other than those expressly provided by the
existing guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the implementing
departments concerned, to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund
so that all of its programmed development projects could be implemented on time to
ensure that intended beneficiaries can avail of the maximum benefits that could be
derived therefrom.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the:
1. Municipal Accountant, to maintain a special account in the General
Fund for development projects from the 20% of the IRA. Also, separate
financial statements should be prepared pursuant to Section 110,
Volume 1 of the New Government Accounting System Manual to
efficiently monitor the actual utilization of the fund; and
2. Municipal Engineering Office, to prepare the Quarterly Report on
Government Publicized Programs, Projects and Activities as set forth by
the existing regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed through the GSO-designate, to:
1. adopt immediate measures as regards the formation of the inventory
committee in order to ascertain the whereabouts and condition of the
properties. Likewise, if the committee had been formed, advise the committee
to regularly perform their designated functions in accordance with what has
been mandated of them in order to ascertain the correct balances as well as the

2. strictly adhere to the guidelines set by the Handbook on Property and Supply
Management and other related issuances on sound practices, such as but not

such from possible mishandling and/or loss of property; and
3. fast track the retrieval of the documents pertaining to the acquisition of PPEs
in prior years in order to start the reconciliation process. In addition, prepare
the appropriate cards of those PPEs that had been identified already in order to
start the compliance process of preparing such monitoring tool.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province collectively and individually ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM responsibilities by enhancing the level of coordination through managing the challenges on the dependencies that affect the essential management processes.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 Proper coordination PDRRMO for the alignment implemented to ensure an effective and efficient delivery of services of the MDRRMO.
observations.reason: Subsumed in the new audit observation as discussed in Part II (Observation No. 1-LDRRM-Procincewide) of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management, through the MENRO, to:
1. fast track the construction of the Municipality’s Sanitary Landfill in compliance with existing Rules and Regulations; 2. as to the programs in minimizing solid wastes, review the strategy for viability, and if warranted, revise the plan to ensure doability and sustainability, taking into consideration the possible hindrances on non-implementation. In addition, check the budget allocation for each program to ensure that enough resources are given to every program; 3. revisit, evaluate, and assess the Municipal Government’s SWMP and have the same aligned with that of the one approved by the authority; and 4. exert extra effort in monitoring the Municipal Government’s compliance with Laws, Rules and Regulations with regards to Solid Waste Management.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 The LGU appropriated funds from the budget for the purchase of land for the sanitary landfill. A provision for Material Recovery Facility and Shredder for the solid wastes was already made. Practices in compliance with the Government's Laws, Rules and Regulations with regard to Solid Waste Management were being slowly applied.
observations.reason: A provision for Material Recovery Facility and Shredder for the solid wastes was already made. The management is still coordinating as to the possible area that is a suitable site for the sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed, through the LSB, to: 1. observe timely preparation and submission of the SEF Annual Budget as provided under the existing Rules and Regulations; 2. improve the processes of planning and budgeting of SEF through: a. instituting measures to make the LSB more proactive and effective in introducing educational reforms within its capacity; and b. the formulation of development programs on education for the Municipal Government, articulating the collective vision of the major stakeholders; 3. enhance the administration and collection of SEF through crafting of an effective tax campaign that could significantly maximize collections in order to improve and make reforms on the quality of education within locality; 4. enhance accountability of the acquired and issued assets and/or properties by conducting regular physical inventory of the same, and evaluating on a timely basis the status and issuance of these subject assets to monitor their whereabouts and serviceability; and 5. revisit the relevant guidelines on SEF and related rules and regulations on property, supply and inventory management.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Local School Board (LSB) was newly reconstituted.
observations.reason: The LSB was newly reconstituted. Some measures implemented but other actions remain ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amounts due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within the prescribed period and to adopt a payment scheme in settling the previous accounts of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Prompt and on-time remittance of the current amount is being adopted; the management implemented a payment scheme in settling the previous accounts of the LGU to said government agencies.
observations.reason: Payment of previous installment basis. Some remittances were made on-time but not all prior amounts were settled within the prescribed reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to reorient themselves on the government procurement and its IRR and ensure that all procurement transactions/activities are in accordance with the prescribed procedures under RA 9184 such as: 1. Ensure that the APP were carefully planned and scheduled; 2. The HOPE (Municipal Mayor) should issue an Executive Order reconstituting the BAC membership including its Secretariat and prescribing their duties and functions pursuant to RA 9184 and its IRR every year; 3. In all instances of procurement, the BAC shall strictly adhere to the advertisement and posting requirements prescribed in Section 21 of RA 9184; 4. Strictly adhere to the guidelines prescribed in Annex H of IRR, RA 9184 for the alternative methods of procurement; and 5. Refrain from purchasing goods through reimbursements and make procurement of purchases in bulk rather than through reimbursements.
observations.managementAction: Directed all department heads to make procurements in accordance with the approved APP. Issuance of an Executive Order reconstituting the BAC including its Secretariat and prescribing their duties and functions pursuant to RA 9184 and its IRR. Directed all offices to adhere to the guidelines prescribed in Annex H of IRR, RA 9184. Directed all offices the refrain from purchasing goods through reimbursements. Posting in conspicuous places in adherence to the advertisement requirements prescribed in Section 21 of RA 9184.
observations.reason: BAC was reconstituted and some directed actions implemented; bulk purchases not yet fully adopted and some procurement issues remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. direct all Office Heads and Drivers to ensure that Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly and completely accomplished; 2. require all Office Heads and Drivers to submit regularly through the Municipal Treasurer, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and monthly report of official travels for proper control and monitoring; 3. instruct the Municipal Accountant who certifies the completeness of the documents to carefully examine all supporting documents before payment for fuel will be effected; 4. direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that issuance of fuels shall be made to vehicles with government plates only; and 5. install internal control system on the issuance of gasoline consumption to avoid reimbursement, through entering into a MOA with a selected supplier and issuance of gas slips for authorized travel only.
observations.managementAction: Directed all concerned offices to ensure Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished. Required offices to submit monthly reports of fuel consumption and travels. Instructed Municipal Accountant to carefully examine supporting documents before payment. Directed Municipal Treasurer to ensure issuance of fuels only to vehicles with government plates. Implemented internal control measures.
observations.reason: Reimbursement through reimbursements were avoided; bulk purchasing yet to be adopted; ongoing implementation of directed controls.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method of accounting for all supplies and materials procured. Also, maintain the subsidiary ledger and cards for each item of inventory; and 2. Municipal Treasurer to maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies and materials; prepare and submit monthly the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Accounting Office for the recording of issuances.
observations.managementAction: Directed the Municipal Accountant to adopt perpetual inventory and maintain subsidiary ledger; instructed Municipal Treasurer to maintain stock cards and submit monthly summaries.
observations.reason: Bulk purchases of office supplies are not yet fully adopted; some purchases continue to be directly charged to expense account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to direct the concerned official to strictly observe Sections 271 and 164 of RA 7160 on remittance of shares on Real Property and Community Taxes and remittance of legally mandated recipients.
observations.managementAction: Prompt remittance of the current amount is being monitored by the government agency and legally mandated recipients; monitoring scheme adopted.
observations.reason: Prompt remittance of current amounts is being implemented but some prior amounts remained outstanding pending monitoring and settlement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. direct all accountable officers to settle all their outstanding cash advances and to stop granting of cash advances to any officials and employees unless the previous ones have been settled; 2. enforce strict compliance to rules and regulations pertaining to grant and utilization of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular 2009-002; 3. the management should exercise diligence in accounting, settlement and monitoring of outstanding cash advances by requiring all accountable officers to refund, submit documents and establish a policy on monitoring of cash advances; and 4. submit to the audit team all liquidation reports concerning the implementation of programs and projects from the cash advances drawn by the municipal officials for both current and prior years.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of Demand Letters requesting settlement of outstanding cash advances; some liquidations were made. Directed employees to comply strictly with rules and regulations. Submission of liquidation reports was requested.
observations.reason: Additional cash advances were still granted despite the existence of previous unliquidated cash advances; some liquidation and recovery actions made but not fully settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to require the Accountant to reconcile ledger balances against Treasurer’s cashbook and bank statements and prepare the necessary cash reconciliation statement pursuant to Section 181(c).
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going; ledger and treasurer's cashbook reconciliation not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to maintain the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for each item as control records and to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records; and 2. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all properties owned by the Municipal Government to ascertain losses, report/reclassify the unserviceable ones, and determine the accuracy, reliability and integrity of the PPE account balances at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Actions subsumed in new audit observation in Part II (Observation No. 4 - PPE) of this report; Management implemented some measures.
observations.reason: Subsumed in the new audit observation as discussed in Part II (Observation No. 4 - PPE) of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred that the Planning and Local Development Council: 1. formulate a detailed Annual Investment Program, incorporating complete description, exact location, estimated cost, and timeframe for completion of each program, project and activity; 2. strictly align projects under 20% Development Fund to the prescribed guidelines under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01; and 3. ensure that the AIP is supported with comprehensive data and information.
observations.managementAction: Actions subsumed in the new audit observation (Part II Observation No. 3 – 20% DF) of this report; implemented some measures.
observations.reason: Subsumed in the new audit observation as discussed in Part II (Observation No. 3 – 20% DF) of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. ensure that LDRRMF is efficiently utilized and that the programs, projects and activities are clearly defined and prioritized in the MDRRMP subject to the provisions of the NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1; and 2. formulate a comprehensive MDRRMP using the NDRRMP template as guide.
observations.managementAction: Actions subsumed in new audit observation as discussed in Part II (Observation No. 2 - LDRRM) of this report; implemented some measures.
observations.reason: Subsumed in the new audit observation as discussed in Part II (Observation No. 2 - LDRRM) of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. immediately stop the granting of cash advances to any officials and employees unless previous cash advances have been settled; 2. stop the practice of implementation of projects through cash advances and follow the proper procurement process as provided under R.A. 9184; 3. exercise diligence in accounting, settlement and monitoring of outstanding cash advances; and 4. submit all liquidation reports concerning implementation of programs and projects from cash advances drawn by municipal officials for current and prior years.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of Demand Letters and requests for settlement; some liquidations done; instructions to stop granting additional advances and to follow procurement rules.
observations.reason: Some liquidations and recovery actions made but unliquidated advances still exist; additional advances were still granted in some instances.
